perturbation.original_text: Party B shall keep in confidence Party A's business secrets of which Party B may be aware on account of Party B's receipt from Party A of the license to use the Program Content. Upon the termination of this Agreement, Party B shall return to Party A or destroy any document, material or software containing such business secrets and delete the same from any memory devices.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B shall endeavor to keep in confidence Party A's business secrets of which Party B may be aware on account of Party B's receipt from Party A of the license to use the Program Content. Upon the termination of this Agreement, Party B should attempt to return to Party A or destroy any document, material or software containing such business secrets and delete the same from any memory devices.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall keep' to 'shall endeavor to keep' weakens the obligation to maintain confidentiality. Similarly, replacing 'shall return' with 'should attempt to return' makes the return or destruction of confidential material less certain. This creates ambiguity about the required level of effort and the definitiveness of the action, making it harder to enforce the confidentiality provision.
perturbation.law_citation: Article 9 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 9 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China mandates the protection of trade secrets, placing a clear obligation on parties to maintain confidentiality. The modified text, by using 'endeavor to keep' and 'should attempt to return', creates ambiguity and lowers the standard of care, potentially falling short of the legal requirement to actively protect trade secrets, which could be interpreted as a violation of Article 9.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE SIX PARTY B'S OBLIGATIONS WITH RESPECT TO CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION 6.2
perturbation.justification: The original contract language ('shall keep,' 'shall return') establishes a clear obligation regarding the confidentiality and return of Party A's business secrets. The changed text uses softer language ('shall endeavor to keep,' 'should attempt to return'), which creates ambiguity about the required level of effort and definitiveness, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation. The Law Explanation claims this weakening contradicts Article 9 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China, which mandates the protection of trade secrets. Although there are no law snippets to confirm this, based on the explanation and definition, the change introduces ambiguity, making the obligation difficult to enforce under state or national law. Thus, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: If Party B becomes aware of any violation of any Intellectual Property Right to the Program Content provided by Party A to Party B, it shall take all measures reasonably necessary to preserve the evidence of such third party violation, notify Party A of the same as soon as reasonably possible, and take actions reasonably requested by Party A to assist in legal actions taken or claims made by Party A in order to protect its Intellectual Property Right.
perturbation.changed_text: If Party B becomes aware of a potential violation of any Intellectual Property Right to the Program Content provided by Party A to Party B, it may consider taking some measures to preserve evidence of such third party violation, notify Party A of the same when practical, and consider taking actions requested by Party A to assist in potential legal actions taken or claims made by Party A in order to protect its Intellectual Property Right.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'any violation' to 'a potential violation,' 'shall take all measures reasonably necessary' to 'may consider taking some measures,' 'notify Party A of the same as soon as reasonably possible' to 'notify Party A of the same when practical,' and 'take actions reasonably requested by Party A' to 'consider taking actions requested by Party A' significantly weakens Party B's obligation to protect Party A's intellectual property rights. The introduction of subjective judgment and discretionary actions contradicts the required active protection and enforcement of IP rights under PRC law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Copyright Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_citation: Article 49 of the Copyright Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 49 of the Copyright Law of the People's Republic of China outlines the legal responsibilities related to copyright infringement, including the obligation to assist in enforcement. By changing the mandatory 'shall' to discretionary 'may consider,' the modified text creates ambiguity and potentially contradicts the obligation to actively protect and assist in the enforcement of copyright as required by Chinese copyright law. This makes it more difficult for Party A to enforce its IP rights, as Party B's assistance becomes discretionary rather than mandatory.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE FIVE INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS TO THE PROGRAM CONTENT 5.3
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: e Law Against Unfair Competition of the PRC (1993). [ citation needed ] The latter prohibited the passing off of registered trademarks, infringing trade secrets, the illegal use of well-known goods or names of other people, as well as other misleading and deceptive conduct. The Advertising Law of the PRC was passed in 1994 and extensively revised in 2015, to now include a joint liability with the advertising spokesperson, special regulations on public interest advertising, and most importantly a definition of misleading advertisement. [ 17 ] The Anti-Monopoly Law provides for investigating unreasonable IP licensing fees and authority for further supplementary regulations on IP issues. [ 10 ] : 278 Geographical indications [ edit ] The use of place names as part of product names to emphasize regional specialties existed in China for over 3,000 years. [ 1 ] : 49 China first encountered European-style geographical indications as intellectual property following Reform and Opening Up in the 1980s. [ 1 ] : 49 Under China's 1984 Trademark Law, geographical names were excluded from trademark registration and in 1986, the State Administration for Industry and Commerce prohibited the use of geographical names of administrative divisions at or above the county levels as trademarks. [ 1 ] : 50 However, regulators in China began protecting foreign geographical indications on a case-by-case basis. [ 1 ] : 51–52 In the 2001 amendments to China's Trademark Law , China adopted the provision on geographical indications from the TRIPS Agreement. [ 1 ] : 53 Under this standard, if a trademark contains a place name but the product does not originate in that area, the geographical name should not be used or registered given the risk of misleading the public. [ 1 ] : 53 A grandfather clause provides an exception where an otherwise prohibited trademark already registered in good faith remains valid. [ 1 ] : 53 The General Administration of Quality Supervision, Inspection and Quarantine (AQSIQ) established a sui generis system to cover the use of geographical indication products through the 2005 Provisions on the Protection of GI Products. [ 1 ] : 53 These regulations establish protection and requirements for products using place names if (1) the product is grown or bred from a certain place name location and all of the raw materials come from that place, (2) products produced elsewhere but for which all of the raw materials come from the place name location, and (3) products where some of the raw materials come from elsewhere but are produced in the place name location using specialized techniques. [ 1 ] : 53–54 This approach is intended to accommodate the use of geographical indications in handicrafts like embroidery or ceramics. [ 1 ] : 54 The regulation sets strict standards for products produced under the geographical indication, and non-co
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens Party B's obligation to protect Party A's intellectual property rights by changing 'shall' to 'may consider' and 'as soon as reasonably possible' to 'when practical.' This contradicts the obligation to actively protect and assist in the enforcement of copyright as required by Chinese copyright law. The original text created a clear obligation for Party B, while the changed text introduces discretion. The scraped snippets mention the Law Against Unfair Competition of the PRC (1993) and the Advertising Law of the PRC, but they don't directly address the specifics of copyright enforcement. However, the Law Explanation mentions Article 49 of the Copyright Law of the People's Republic of China, which outlines the obligation to assist in enforcement. Therefore, the modified text introduces ambiguity and contradicts the obligation to actively protect and assist in the enforcement of copyright.

perturbation.original_text: Party B warrants that it will take all technical methods and confidential measures reasonably available to Party B to ensure that only Party A and certain of Party B personnel designated by Party A may have access to the Program Content licensed by Party A to Party B. Without Party A's permission in writing, Party B may not disclose or sublicense the Program Content to any third party, except for the Program Content related to Party B Business.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B intends to take some technical methods and confidential measures to generally ensure that only Party A and some of Party B personnel have access to the Program Content licensed by Party A to Party B. Without explicit Party A's permission, Party B might disclose or sublicense the Program Content to some third parties, especially for the Program Content related to Party B Business.
perturbation.explanation: The modification significantly weakens Party B's warranty regarding the protection of Program Content. Replacing 'warrants that it will take all technical methods and confidential measures reasonably available' with 'intends to take some technical methods and confidential measures' introduces ambiguity and reduces the assurance of robust security. Changing 'to ensure that only Party A and certain of Party B personnel designated by Party A may have access' to 'to generally ensure that only Party A and some of Party B personnel have access' further weakens the control over access. Removing the strict prohibition on disclosure and sublicensing, and replacing it with 'might disclose or sublicense' alongside the vague exception 'especially for the Program Content related to Party B Business' opens the door to potential breaches of confidentiality, weakening the protection of trade secrets, which is governed by PRC law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: The Law of the People's Republic of China on Guarding State Secrets
perturbation.law_citation: Article 48 of the Law of the People's Republic of China on Guarding State Secrets
perturbation.law_explanation: Although this agreement concerns content licensing, the underlying principles relate to the protection of sensitive information. Article 48 of the Law of the People's Republic of China on Guarding State Secrets is related to the protection of confidential information. By weakening the guarantee to protect access to the licensed program content and opening possibilities of disclosure to unspecified third parties, the revised text becomes inconsistent with the necessary protective obligations regarding state secrets. This makes it harder to determine whether violations are occurring.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE SIX PARTY B'S OBLIGATIONS WITH RESPECT TO CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION 6.3

Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Notice of the Supreme People's Court, the Ministry of Justice and the China Banking Regulatory Commission on Maximizing the Enforceability of Notarial Certificates to Serve the Risk Prevention and Control of Financial Claims of Banking Financial Institutions [Effective] 最高人民法院、司法部、中国银监会关于充分发挥公证书的强制执行效力服务银行金融债权风险防控的通知 [现行有效] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Supreme People's Court , Instrumentalities of the State Council , All Ministries , Ministry of Justice , All Commissions , China Banking Regulatory Commission Document Number： No. 76 [2017] of the Ministry of Justice Date issued： 07-13-2017 Effective date: 07-13-2017 Level of Authority: Documents of Judicial Interpretation Nature Area of Law: Banking & Finance Notice of the Supreme People's Court, the Ministry of Justice and the China Banking Regulatory Commission on Maximizing the Enforceability of Notarial Certificates to Serve the Risk Prevention and Control of Financial Claims of Banking Financial Institutions 最高人民法院、司法部、中国银监会关于充分发挥公证书的强制执行效力服务银行金融债权风险防控的通知 (No. 76 [2017] of the Ministry of Justice) （司发通〔2017〕76号） The higher people's courts and justice departments (bureaus) of all provinces, autonomous regions, and municipalities directly under the Central Government; the Military Court of the People's Liberation Army; the Production and Construction Corps Branch of the Higher People's Court of Xinjiang Uygur Autonomous Region and the Justice Bureau of Xinjiang Production and Construction Corps; all local offices of the China Banking Regulatory Commission (“CBRC”); all policy banks, large banks, and joint stock banks; Postal Savings Bank of China; foreign-funded banks; financial asset management companies; and other relevant financial institutions: 各省、自治区、直辖市高级人民法院、司法厅（局），解放军军事法院，新疆维吾尔自治区高级人民法院生产建设兵团分院、新疆生产建设兵团司法局；各银监局，各政策性银行、大型银行、股份制银行，邮储银行，外资银行，金融资产管理公司，其他有 关金融机构： For the purposes of further strengthening the prevention and control of financial risks, maximizing the preventive role of the legal regime of notarization, improving the efficiency of satisfaction of financial claims of banking financial institutions, reducing the costs of satisfaction of financial claims, and effectively raising the risk prevention and control level of banking financial institutions, you are hereby notified of the following requirements for maximizing the enforceability of notarial certificates in the operations of banking financial institutions to serve the risk prevention and control of their financial claims: 为进一步加强金融风险防控，充分发挥公证作为预防性法律制度的作用，提高银行业金融机构金融债权实现效率，降低金融债权实现成本，有效提高银行业金融机构防控风险的水平，现就在银行业金融机构经营业务中进一步发挥公证书的强制执行效力，服务银行金融债权风险防控通知如下： I. Notaries public may grant enforceability to the following debt instruments that are signed by banking financial institutions in their operations in compliance with Article 37 of the Notary Law : 一、 公证机构可以对银行业金融机构运营中所签署的符合 《 公证法 》第 37条 规定的以下债权文书赋予强制执行效力： (1) Various financing contracts, including but not limited to various loan contracts such as credit contracts, borrowing contracts, entrusted loan contracts, and trust loan contracts, various note financing contracts such as bill acceptance agreements, financial leasing contracts, factoring contracts, contracts on issuance of letters of credit, and credit card financing contracts (including credit card contracts and various installment contracts). （一）各类融资合同，包括各类授信合同，借款合同、委托贷款合同、信托贷款合同等各类贷款合同，票据承兑协议等各类票据融资合同，融资租赁合同，保理合同，开立信用证合同，信用卡融资合同（包括信用卡合约及各类分期付款合同）等； (2) Debt restructuring contracts, repayment contracts, and re
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguities that weaken the protection of Program Content, contradicting the intention to protect sensitive information. The original text provides a warranty that specific technical methods and confidential measures will be taken to protect the content. The changed text only suggests that Party B 'intends to take some technical methods and confidential measures', which significantly reduces the assurance of security and increases the possibility of disclosure to third parties. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 is missing, and Scraped Law Snippet 2 discusses enforceability of notarial certificates related to financial claims, it shows that financial institutions use documentation to serve risk prevention. In this content licensing agreement, the ambiguous legal obligation weakens the enforceability of the original text and weakens the intention to protect sensitive information.
